coastal plants **Plants with hibiscus-like flowers on the shores
How to tell them apart?
updated Aug 09**

These two plants look very similar at first glance. Here’s more on how to tell them apart.

Sea hibiscus(Hibiscus tiliceaus)

Maroon stigma (the sticky tip
of the columnar structure
in the middle of the flower).

Fruit smaller and splits when ripe.

Leaves thinner
with less obvious veins.

Baru-baru(Thespesia polpunea)

Yellow stigma.

Fruit larger and does not split.

Leaves thicker, more leathery,
with more obvious yellow veins.

www.flickr.com
